title: scaevola revoluta subsp. stenostachya
intro: Scaevola revoluta subsp. stenostachya, a species of flowering plant in the family Goodeniaceae, is a native Australian plant commonly known as the "fan flower" or "fan-flower." Traditionally, the plant has been used in Aboriginal medicine, and it is believed to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions, due to its bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. In herbal preparations, Scaevola revoluta subsp. stenostachya is often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications to harness its therapeutic potential.
